commands.repair_reason: The code repair is now concrete, but merge should wait for fresh current-head proof and maintainer confirmation of the terminal delivery semantics.; Cleared: The diff changes subagent delivery bookkeeping and tests only; it adds no dependencies, CI execution, permissions, credential handling, or external command surface. (sha=b84c800f6bad93d2d1213f2028a74b4cb1dd6596)
commands.review_summary:
The PR separates subagent requester wake success from required visible message-tool delivery failure metadata across announce delivery, lifecycle persistence, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +55. Total +126 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main returns `delivered: false` for required message-tool no-evidence completion delivery, while the PR changes that path to requester wake delivered plus visible failure metadata. I did not run a live transport reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The code repair is now concrete, but merge should wait for fresh current-head proof and maintainer confirmation of the terminal delivery semantics.

target.title: fix(subagents): preserve requester wake on visible delivery failure
target.body:
## Summary
- split requester wake success from visible message-tool delivery status for subagent completions
- treat requester-agent handoff as delivered when it runs but fails to produce required message-tool output, while recording `visibleDeliveryStatus: failed`
- persist visible completion delivery failure details on subagent run records without leaving the terminal result in pending final delivery retry state

## Why
For group/channel subagent completions, visible replies must still go through the message tool. When that visible delivery fails, the requester/parent wake should remain visible to orchestration instead of becoming a passive `pendingFinalDelivery` dead letter. This keeps the safety policy intact while preserving parent continuation.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** subagent completion delivery where the requester/parent handoff runs but the required group/channel visible reply does not produce message-tool evidence.

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, using the patched subagent announce delivery and registry lifecycle modules with a redacted runtime proof harness. The harness used an active requester session shape, a group/channel route configured as `message_tool_only`, and copied live terminal output from the run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran `node --import tsx <redacted-temp-proof-script>` from the PR checkout after applying commit `9a7bbc68d3`.

**Evidence after fix:** copied terminal output from the redacted local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "proof": "pr-85308 requester wake survives visible delivery failure",
  "scenario": {
    "requester": "active parent session",
    "visibleRoute": "group/channel completion configured as message_tool_only",
    "inducedVisibleFailure": "announce agent returned no message-tool delivery evidence",
    "expectedOutcome": "requester wake is credited as delivered while visible delivery failure metadata remains recorded"
  },
  "deliveryResults": [
    {
      "delivered": true,
      "path": "direct",
      "requesterWakeStatus": "delivered",
      "visibleDeliveryRequired": true,
      "visibleDeliveryStatus": "failed",
      "visibleDeliveryError": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
      "error": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
      "phases": [
        {
          "phase": "direct-primary",
          "delivered": true,
          "path": "direct",
          "error": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
          "requesterWakeStatus": "delivered",
          "visibleDeliveryRequired": true,
          "visibleDeliveryStatus": "failed",
          "visibleDeliveryError": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "keyAssertions": {
    "requesterWakeDeliveredAt": true,
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryRequired": true,
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryStatus": "failed",
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryError": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
    "completionAnnouncedAt": true,
    "completionDeliveredAt": true,
    "pendingFinalDeliveryCleared": true,
    "cleanupCompleted": true,
    "noUnexpectedResume": true,
    "noFallbackSendMessage": true
  },
  "finalRunRecordFields": {
    "completionDeliveredAt": 1779447271405,
    "completionAnnouncedAt": 1779447271405,
    "requesterWakeDeliveredAt": 1779447271405,
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryRequired": true,
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryStatus": "failed",
    "visibleCompletionDeliveryError": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
    "lastAnnounceDeliveryError": "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool",
    "pendingFinalDelivery": null,
    "cleanupCompletedAt": 1779447271405
  }
}
```

The proof also captured the requester handoff call using `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` with redacted session keys and no fallback `sendMessage` call.

**Observed result after fix:** requester-agent handoff returned `delivered: true` and recorded `requesterWakeStatus: "delivered"` while visible completion delivery stayed explicitly recorded as `visibleCompletionDeliveryStatus: "failed"` with `visibleCompletionDeliveryError: "completion agent did not deliver through the message tool"`. The final run record had `completionDeliveredAt`, `completionAnnouncedAt`, and `requesterWakeDeliveredAt` set; `pendingFinalDelivery` was cleared; cleanup completed; and no fallback direct `sendMessage` path ran.

**What was not tested:** this proof did not send a live Slack/Discord message to an external channel. It intentionally forced the same required-message-tool no-evidence failure path in a redacted local runtime proof so the PR can show requester wake and persisted failure metadata without posting to a real workspace.

## Tests
- `CI=true corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts -t 'preserves requester wake|records visible delivery|records requester wake separately'`
- `corepack pnpm format:check src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.types.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## Notes
- A broader full-file Vitest run reached 167/168 passing but timed out in an existing Telegram failure-path test that waits out the announce retry timeout.
- Plain `tsc --noEmit` exhausted Node heap on this checkout; `tsgo` checks passed instead.
target.author: dolencluka

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
